meaning: n.演说,故事,饶舌v.喋喋不休地高谈阔论,演奏音乐
examples: He was sure that the line wasn't part of the man's spiel.
Do not just spout marketing spiel or you will quickly lose followers.
The team has learned the power of lowering expectations and given Yume a new spiel.
You've got three minutes to get through your spiel and send it off to the recipient.
Of course, almost anything would be an improvement from Jeff Zucker's awkward--and long--digital spiel last year.
